summary: Add explicit M3 runtime save/load support
acceptance_criteria: M3 runtime save/load explicitly preserves polity, suzerain chain, obligations/audits, administrative burden inputs/state, characters, relationships, offices, appointments, policies, postwar arrangement state, and succession state needed by accepted M3 tasks.
Same seed, same fixture, same command sequence, and same saved M3 snapshot produce the same state hash after load as before save in the Node path.
Existing M1/M2 save/load golden behavior and corrupt/future/cross-manifest rejection behavior remain compatible.
Malformed or missing M3 save data is rejected with stable reason codes/paths before runtime replacement and without partial state mutation.

DTO/schema changes remain explicit, serializable, versioned, and confined to save-format/sim-core/protocol boundaries; no platform IO, IndexedDB, Electron IPC, compression, database, object graph serialization, or new production dependency is introduced.
If M3 implementation chooses not to support save/load for a new state slice, it must return a structured unsupported-save rejection and M3 exit cannot claim persistence support for that slice.
required_tests: Save round-trip tests cover M3 polity/obligation/character/office/policy/postwar/succession state and malformed/future/cross-manifest rejection.
node -e JSON parse/shape check for M3-SAVE-RUNTIME-001
notes: This task exists because M3 introduces explicit state and schema slices; it is not a platform persistence task.
If save-format changes would alter frozen D-011 rather than extending explicit DTO/schema support, stop and route to systems_architect.
Do not implement IndexedDB, Electron filesystem saves, migrations beyond the accepted explicit save contract, cloud saves, or compression.
